nodes.x: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.
Peter answered and said unto him, Though all men shall be offended because of thee, yet will I never be offended.
And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
But Peter said unto him, Although all shall be offended, yet will not I.
I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.
And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name’s sake.
Behold, hath he commanded any that they should depart out of the synagogues, or out of the houses of worship? Behold, I say unto you, Nay.
They said in their hearts, Let us destroy them together: they have burned up all the synagogues of God in the land.
Behold I say unto you, do ye suppose that ye cannot worship God save it be in your synagogues only?

Nevertheless, ye shall not cast him out of your synagogues, or your places of worship, for unto such shall ye continue to minister; for ye know not but what they will return and repent, and come unto me with full purpose of heart, and I shall heal them; and ye shall be the means of bringing salvation unto them.
And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.
This much did the Father command me, that I should tell unto them:
But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
And inasmuch as they are faithful unto me, it shall be made known unto them what they shall do;
And they shall believe in me, that I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God, and shall pray unto the Father in my name.
But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.
These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.

And I exhort you to remember these things; for the time speedily cometh that ye shall know that I lie not, for ye shall see me at the bar of God; and the Lord God will say unto you: Did I not declare my words unto you, which were written by this man, like as one crying from the dead, yea, even as one speaking out of the dust?
And said unto them, I see your father’s countenance, that it is not toward me as before; but the God of my father hath been with me.
But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?
And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
Then I sent unto him, saying, There are no such things done as thou sayest, but thou feignest them out of thine own heart.
And he answered me and said, Knowest thou not what these be? And I said, No, my lord.
And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

My heart hath been weighed down with sorrow from time to time, for I have feared, lest for the hardness of your hearts the Lord your God should come out in the fulness of his wrath upon you, that ye be cut off and destroyed forever;
Verily I say unto you, there have been some few things in thine heart and with thee with which I, the Lord, was not well pleased.
Behold, verily I say unto you, that these are the words of the Lord your God.

O then, if I have seen so great things, if the Lord in his condescension unto the children of men hath visited men in so much mercy, why should my heart weep and my soul linger in the valley of sorrow, and my flesh waste away, and my strength slacken, because of mine afflictions?
And they began to be sorrowful, and to say unto him one by one, Is it I? and another said, Is it I?
Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.
But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:
And I will send upon him the Comforter, which shall teach him the truth and the way whither he shall go;
I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.

Wherefore, I now send upon you another Comforter, even upon you my friends, that it may abide in your hearts, even the Holy Spirit of promise; which other Comforter is the same that I promised unto my disciples, as is recorded in the testimony of John.

3. Wherefore, I now send upon you another Comforter, even upon you
    my friends, that it may abide in your hearts, even the Holy Spirit
    of promise; which other Comforter is the same that I promised unto
    my disciples, as is recorded in the testimony of John.
And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
And he shall rise the third day from the dead; and behold, he standeth to judge the world; and behold, all these things are done that a righteous judgment might come upon the children of men.

And he shall come into the world to redeem his people; and he shall take upon him the transgressions of those who believe on his name; and these are they that shall have eternal life, and salvation cometh to none else.
To earth he will come when beneath the whole heaven The Kingdom and Judgment to Saints will be given.
And let him repent of his sins, for he seeketh the praise of the world.
And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
Of sin, because they believe not on me;
And by this you may know they are under the bondage of sin, because they come not unto me.
That they teach you not to do after all their abominations, which they have done unto their gods; so should ye sin against the Lord your God.
They eat up the sin of my people, and they set their heart on their iniquity.
Woe to the rebellious children, saith the Lord, that take counsel, but not of me; and that cover with a covering, but not of my spirit, that they may add sin to sin:
How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.
Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;

righteousness in all our ways, that it may rule in us until sin and its consequences shall be subdued, and we gain a victory even over death and the grave, and life eternal shall reign supreme upon the face of the whole earth. I ask God to add his blessing in the name of Jesus Christ. Amen.
For a permanent and everlasting establishment and order unto my church, to advance the cause, which ye have espoused, to the salvation of man, and to the glory of your Father who is in heaven;
So that I come again to my father’s house in peace; then shall the Lord be my God:
Being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.

Stand, therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, having on the breastplate of righteousness, and your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace, which I have sent mine angels to commit unto you;
Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.
Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.
And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
Say unto them, As truly as I live, saith the Lord, as ye have spoken in mine ears, so will I do to you:
But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to…
And again, he that receiveth the word of truth, doth he receive it by the Spirit of truth or some other way?

But he that believeth these things which I have spoken, him will I visit with the manifestations of my Spirit, and he shall know and bear record. For because of my Spirit he shall know that these things are true; for it persuadeth men to do good.
The truth comes to man by means of higher intelligences; by the voice of God— by the ministering of angels, and by the Holy Spirit of prophecy and revelation.
He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
And saith unto him, All these things will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.
Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation aright will I shew the salvation of God.
And said unto me, Thou art my servant, O Israel, in whom I will be glorified.
All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
And he that receiveth my Father receiveth my Father’s kingdom; therefore all that my Father hath shall be given unto him.
And whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, which is right, believing that ye shall receive, behold it shall be given unto you.
And now I go unto the Father. And verily I say unto you, whatsoever things ye shall ask the Father in my name shall be given unto you.
A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.

Then said some of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?

Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye inquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.

Then said some of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the…
A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.
And he said unto the disciples, The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it.
¶ And he turned him unto his disciples, and said privately, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see:

For I say unto you, that ye shall not see me henceforth and know that I am he of whom it is written by the prophets, until ye shall say: Blessed is he who cometh in the name of the Lord, in the clouds of heaven, and all the holy angels with him. Then understood his disciples that he should come again on the earth, after that he was glorified and crowned on the right hand of God.
They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.
¶ Who is he that saith, and it cometh to pass, when the Lord commandeth it not?
(And when they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)
He that saith, I know him, and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
That say: Let him make speed, hasten his work, that we may see it; and let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw nigh and come, that we may know it.
Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye inquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall…

And Jesus again showed himself unto them, for they were praying unto the Father in his name; and Jesus came and stood in the midst of them, and said unto them: What will ye that I shall give unto you?
Then said Jesus again unto them, I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.
And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, What will ye that I shall do unto you?
Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
Rejoice, O my heart, and cry unto the Lord, and say: O Lord, I will praise thee forever; yea, my soul will rejoice in thee, my God, and the rock of my salvation.
And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.
Behold, my servants shall sing for joy of heart, but ye shall cry for sorrow of heart, and shall howl for vexation of spirit.
And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.
If thou art sorrowful, call on the Lord thy God with supplication, that your souls may be joyful.
A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.
The sorrows of a travailing woman shall come upon him: he is an unwise son; for he should not stay long in the place of the breaking forth of children.
Before she travailed, she brought forth; before her pain came, she was delivered of a man child.
And she being with child cried, travailing in birth, and pained to be delivered.
Like as a woman with child, that draweth near the time of her delivery, is in pain, and crieth out in her pangs; so have we been in thy sight, O Lord.
Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said, There is a man child conceived.
And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.
Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory rejoiceth: my flesh also shall rest in hope.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
I sent him therefore the more carefully, that, when ye see him again, ye may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.
But behold, ye are gone, and my sorrows cannot bring your return.
But rejoice, inasmuch as ye are partakers of Christ’s sufferings; that, when his glory shall be revealed, ye may be glad also with exceeding joy.
And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.
At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:
And in that day whatsoever any man shall ask, it shall be given unto him.
Whatsoever ye ask the Father in my name it shall be given unto you, that is expedient for you;
Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.
Wherefore, fear not even unto death; for in this world your joy is not full, but in me your joy is full.
And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.
And these things write we unto you, that your joy may be full.
These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.
His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.
Therefore say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; There shall none of my words be prolonged any more, but the word which I have spoken shall be done, saith the Lord God.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, when these things shall be made known unto them of the Father, and shall come forth of the Father, from them unto you;
I give unto you these sayings that you may understand and know how to worship, and know what you worship, that you may come unto the Father in my name, and in due time receive of his fulness.
At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:
Therefore ye must always pray unto the Father in my name;
For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.
Jesus said unto them, If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me.
But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.
He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.
Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.
As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.
I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.
And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one, as we are.
And it came to pass that the voice of the Lord came unto my father, that we should arise and go down into the ship.
All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
And now I go unto the Father, because it is expedient that I should go unto the Father for your sakes.
His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.
¶ Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
Peter said unto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not deny thee. Likewise also said all the disciples.
¶ And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.
But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee.

And the man said unto him: We do not believe that thou knowest any such thing. We do not believe in these foolish traditions. We do not believe that thou knowest of things to come, neither do we believe that thy fathers and also that our fathers did know concerning the things which they spake, of that which is to come.
Yea, I tell thee, that thou mayest know that there is none else save God that knowest thy thoughts and the intents of thy heart.
Now I ask, is this faith? Behold, I say unto you, Nay; for if a man knoweth a thing he hath no cause to believe, for he knoweth it.
Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?
Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me.
Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?
And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
And he said, My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.
Therefore be ye also ready, for in such an hour as ye think not, the Son of Man cometh.
Behold, the days come, that all that is in thine house, and that which thy fathers have laid up in store until this day, shall be carried to Babylon: nothing shall be left, saith the Lord.
Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.
Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.
These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
And not only so, but we glory in tribulations also: knowing that tribulation worketh patience;

nodes.lb: GC 1972/04 — Elder Eldred G. Smith Patriarch to the Church In that upper room in Jerusalem where the Last Supper was held, Jesus gave his disciples considerable instructions. Among many things he taught them, he said: “Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.” ( John 14:27 .) Again he said: “These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.” ( John 16:33 .) Peace is a much-used word nowadays. We hear it on every side, in every newspaper, in every magazine. Men are truly running to and fro all over the earth looking for peace. We think of it as a modern form of salutation, but it is as old as mankind. The people of biblical lands have always greeted each other with “Peace be unto you” or “Peace be with you.” Yet that little section
nodes.x: Peace By Elder Eldred G. Smith Patriarch to the Church In that upper room in Jerusalem where the Last Supper was held, Jesus gave his disciples considerable instructions. Among many things he taught them, he said: “Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.” ( John 14:27 .) Again he said:…
Go and cry unto the gods which ye have chosen; let them deliver you in the time of your tribulation.
